courses.name: Operating Systems w/ Advanced Projects
courses.description: I IA for this course! I learned quite a bit about concurrency, networking, and especially virtual memory. However, I learned a lot more about debugging. A lot about debugging, especially the incredible value of spamming as many assertions as humanly possible + fuzzing.

courses.name: Advanced Compilers
courses.description: A fun continuation of EECS 483. Learning LLVM was cool, and partially vibecoding the final project was also fun.

courses.name: Parallel Programming
courses.description: I think the course was interesting from the perspective of learning a bit about how to program GPUs in CUDA, but the course structure and teaching left much to be desired in my opinion.

courses.name: Compiler Construction
courses.description: I developed a compiler over the semester to go from a high-level, functional programming language to SSA, and then to Assembly. Conceptually heavy at times, but a really fun and interesting class that has furthered my interest in this field.

courses.name: Web Systems
courses.description: The first half was familiar to me, but I still learned some new things and enjoyed it. I took a lot of interest in the distributed systems/MapReduce in the second half, and have since wanted to explore this area more.

courses.name: Game Design and Development
courses.description: An infinite but fun time sink during an already busy semester, but a rewarding and fun class. I'm really proud of the games we made, and I learned quite a bit about game development. One of the most important takeaways for me though was thinking about how to guide users and the value of play testing sessions. Both are quite practical for building tech in the real world!

courses.name: Foundations of Computer Science
courses.description: I really enjoyed the content of this class, especially algorithms and reducations, but the class was nevertheless quite challenging for me.

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Organization
courses.description: Projects were pretty interesting, especially the linker. This course helped me understand a bit more about what's going on inside a computer. Three main takeaways: branch prediction, struct alignment, and caching solves all problems.

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: I IAed for this course for several semesters! Speedrunning the projects was pretty fun, and the course content is fairly interesting and useful.

courses.name: Computer Science Pragmatics
courses.description: Cool class; I definitely learned things about Git, and it carved the way for me to become an Nvim enjoyer
